qa_pairs.question: How many specialized software programs are available?
qa_pairs.answer: More than 900 software programs and packages are already integrated with national compute clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What type of storage solutions are offered?
qa_pairs.answer: Robust storage solutions are available for backup and mid- to long-term storage.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of training is provided?
qa_pairs.answer: Training sessions covering core skills are offered regularly, and custom courses are available upon request.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the general training calendar?
qa_pairs.answer: The training calendar is available at https://alliancecan.ca/en/training.

qa_pairs.question: Are there specific training resources for ACENET?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, ACENET Training Resources can be found at https://www.ace-net.ca/training/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I access training offered by Calcul Québec?
qa_pairs.answer: Calcul Québec Training is available at https://www.calculquebec.ca/en/academic-research-services/training/.

qa_pairs.question: Does Compute Ontario provide training opportunities?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, training information for Compute Ontario is available at https://www.computeontario.ca/training-overview.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find training events for Western Canada Research Computing?
qa_pairs.answer: Western Canada Research Computing training events are listed at https://training.westdri.ca/blog/.

qa_pairs.question: Which editions of the HSS Winter Series are available?
qa_pairs.answer: The HSS Winter Series has editions for 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the 2024 HSS Winter Series information?
qa_pairs.answer: The 2024 HSS Winter Series can be accessed at https://hss24.netlify.app/.

qa_pairs.question: Is there support for visualization needs?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a dedicated 3D visualization expert is available for support.

qa_pairs.question: Can I run Windows applications using cloud services?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can run Windows applications in the cloud.

qa_pairs.question: What are some additional resources for Canadian Digital Humanities Scholars?
qa_pairs.answer: Other resources include the Alliance of Digital Humanities Organizations (ADHO), the Canadian Society for Digital Humanities (CSDH), the Digital Humanities Summer Institute (DHSI), and HSS Commons.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for the Canadian Society for Digital Humanities (CSDH)?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL for the Canadian Society for Digital Humanities (CSDH) is https://csdh-schn.org/.

qa_pairs.question: What projects are highlighted in the Project Showcase?
qa_pairs.answer: The Project Showcase features Archives Unleashed, the Canadian Writing Research Collaboratory (CWRC), and Voyant.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more details about the Canadian Writing Research Collaboratory (CWRC)?
qa_pairs.answer: More information about the Canadian Writing Research Collaboratory (CWRC) is available at https://cwrc.ca/.
